ble 4 ROWS: J = P × Cd − Cr . | 1 --- EXPERIMENT CONDITION CONTEXT (source=html_remote) --- - c donor. The skin was stored at −80°C. Prior to each permeation experiment; the skins were allowed to thaw at room temperature. After washing and equilibration with PBS, the skin was mounted on static vertical Franz Diffusion cells—Permegear Inc., Bethlehem, PA (receptor volume 5.1 ml, donor area 0.64 cm 2 ) by clamping them between the donor and receptor compartments. The receptor compartment was filled with PBS (pH - ng

, donor area 0.64 cm 2 ) by clamping them between the donor and receptor compartments. The receptor compartment was filled with PBS (pH 7.4) and maintained at 37 ± 0.5°C with constant stirring at 600 RPM. Formulations were added to the donor compartment as an infinite dose - for the gel formulations was determined using Fick’s law J = P × Cd − Cr . 1 where, J is the flux per unit area, P is the permeability coefficient, and Cd and Cr are the concentrations of drug in the donor and receptor solutions, respectively.

, donor area 0.64 cm 2 ) by clamping them between the donor and receptor compartments. The receptor compartment was filled with PBS (pH 7.4) and maintained at 37 ± 0.5°C with constant stirring at 600 RPM. Formulations were added to the donor compartment as an infinite dose - for the gel formulations was determined using Fick’s law J = P × Cd − Cr . 1 where, J is the flux per unit area, P is the permeability coefficient, and Cd and Cr are the concentrations of drug in the donor and receptor solutions, respectively.

provenance.route_notes: Study examines the efficiency of nanoparticle-based formulations for improving ibuprofen permeability through skin.
